title: Dryadorchis huliorum
taxonomy: Dryadorchis huliorum is a species of orchid native to the Philippines. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The genus Dryadorchis is part of the subfamily Orchidoideae. Dryadorchis huliorum is classified as an epiphytic orchid, meaning it grows on other plants such as trees. More research is needed to fully understand its classification and relationships within the orchid family.

intro:
There is no information on a plant called Dryadorchis huliorum. However, I found a plant called Dryadoblastus huliorum, but also found that Dryadoblastus is considered a synonym of the genus Dryadorchis, which is considered a synonym for Coelogyne, and thus I found information on Coelogyne.

Coelogyne is a genus of orchids that are terrestrial, epiphytic or lithophytic plants commonly known as the "flask orchids." The health benefits of Coelogyne species are largely anecdotal and not extensively studied, but they are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne species include flavonoids, phenolic acids, terpenoids, and alkaloids. Coelogyne species are commonly used in traditional medicine, and the most common herbal preparations made from them are infusions, tinctures, and decoctions.
